headline: Macron opens the door to 'reparations' for slavery
description: With one year left in his term, the French president launched a new chapter in France's reckoning with its past by addressing, for the first time, the issue of 'reparations' linked to the transatlantic slave trade.

headline: War in Ukraine: Kherson and Oleshky, two worlds face to face just 10 kilometers apart
Ebola in the DRC: 'The epidemic is unprecedented in scale'
How France's pre-deportation centers became an 'extension of the prison system'
description: The rate of deportation for undocumented foreigners held in administrative detention centers has never been so low, even as detention periods have grown longer and conditions have deteriorated.

headline: Trump eases restrictions on super-pollutants used in air-conditioning and refrigeration
description: The US President announced the easing of curbs on hydrofluorocarbons (HFCs). Trump said the move would reduce the cost of living for US consumers. His approval ratings are plummeting as disruption to oil supplies from the Iran war drives up prices.

headline: 'In a country as polarized as Spain, it is Pedro Sánchez's international stance that garners the most support'
description: In this op-ed for Le Monde, writer Daniel Gascón explains that the Spanish prime minister's positions on Donald Trump's policies and the situation in the Middle East have drawn fierce criticism from foreign leaders. Yet Sánchez's assertiveness on the world stage stands in sharp contrast to his weakness at home.

headline: From Italy to Libya, the dehumanizing processing of migrants
description: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (4/4). Exploited, extorted and abused in Libya, exiles who survive the crossing are rendered invisible and sometimes fall into the hands of mafias linked to agricultural businesses. But 'what matters is that irregular arrivals decrease,' as an official in Rome put it.

'Team Musul': The French youths convicted of attempting to join IS in Mozambique
description: Six young French nationals were convicted in Paris for planning to migrate to Mozambique together and join the IS-affiliated jihadist group al-Shabab in Cabo Delgado province, where it has been active since 2017.

headline: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
description: France is experiencing a record number of data breaches. Despite the damage caused by these hacks, the level of cybersecurity in businesses and government agencies remains inadequate.

headline: How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
description: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.
